pi_items.source: Consistent with task description (case 36 revision) and environment setup (Docker user account defined in env.json)
Task description explicitly states: 'The implant is currently placed vertically and at the crest level' - vertical placement equals 0 degrees angulation
Task description explicitly states: 'Tilt the implant approximately 10 degrees lingually'. Research confirms 10° is within clinically accepted range for tilted implants (11°-45°)

Research literature confirms 5.0° is a standard benchmark for surgical guide accuracy. Studies show mean angular deviations of 5.0° ± 1.6° for bone-supported guides and 5.46° ± 2.41° for mucosa-supported guides
Task description explicitly states: 'Move the implant 2mm deeper (apically) into the bone'
Research shows guided surgery accuracy at platform ranges from 0.33-1.0 mm horizontally and 0.76-0.98 mm at apex. 1.0 mm represents upper bound of typical clinical tolerance for depth measurement
FDI World Dental Federation notation system: first digit 3 = lower left quadrant, second digit 6 = first molar. Verified against Wikipedia and dental reference sources
Web search confirms current version is Blue Sky Plan V5.0 64bit. Official BlueSkyBio website and documentation
Direct inspection of DICOM files in /examples/blue_sky_plan_env/data/dicom/. All files contain phantom data (Patient Name: DENTAL^PHANTOM, Patient ID: BSP_TEST_001, Study Date: 20260216)

privileged_info_summary: The modify_implant_parameters task uses Blue Sky Plan 5.0 software to modify a dental implant at site #36 (lower left first molar). The task requires tilting an initially vertical implant 10 degrees lingually and moving it 2mm deeper into bone. Tolerance values of 5 degrees for angulation and 1.0mm for depth are consistent with standard surgical guide accuracy literature. The DICOM dataset is synthetic phantom data (100 slices, patient name DENTAL^PHANTOM) rather than real patient data. All numerical parameters (initial angulation 0°, target angulation 10°, depth change 2.0mm) are explicitly verified against the task description. The project file path and file naming convention are consistent with Windows environment setup.
